template.name: Node

location.label: Each node starts in this commited state to force it to pick a random timeout immediately

location.name: StableLeader
location.label:
The machine moves to this state once it has sent enough heartbeats to safely say it is a stable leader.
This is to make the state space finite, which allows the model checker to verify it.

transition.label: rand:int[ETL,ETU]
timer == timeout && votes[id] < ((N/2)+1)
requestVote!
requesterId:=id, currentTerm:=(++currentTerm), requestVoteTerm:=currentTerm, votes[id]:=1, voted:=true, timer:=0, timeout:=rand
A new timeout is randomly selected each time the the Candidate starts a new term to prevent repeated split votes.
heartbeat?
timer:=0, currentTerm:= max(currentTerm, heartbeatTerm), voted:=false

Follower's election timeout resets when it receives a heartbeat from Leader.
Also, voted is set to false in prep for the next term.
timer >= timeout
requestVote!
requesterId:=id, currentTerm:=(++currentTerm), requestVoteTerm:=currentTerm, votes[id]:=1, voted:=true, timer:=0
(voted == false) || (requestVoteTerm >= currentTerm)
requestVote?
votes[requesterId]++, voted:=true, currentTerm:= max(currentTerm, requestVoteTerm), timer:=0
rand:int[HBL,HBU]
votes[id]>=((N/2)+1) && timer <= timeout
heartbeatTerm=max(heartbeatTerm, currentTerm), timeout:=rand, votes[id]:=0, voted:=false, timer:=0, heartbeatCounter:=0

query.formula: ((node0.Leader + node1.Leader + node2.Leader + node3.Leader + node4.Leader) == 2) --> ((node0.StableLeader + node1.StableLeader + node2.StableLeader + node3.StableLeader + node4.StableLeader) == 1)
query.comment: If there are two leaders, then eventually there will be only one StableLeader.

query.formula: E<> node0.StableLeader + node1.StableLeader + node2.StableLeader + node3.StableLeader + node4.StableLeader == 1
query.comment: It is possible for some node to become the stable leader.

query.formula: A[] deadlock imply ( (node0.StableLeader + node1.StableLeader + node2.StableLeader + node3.StableLeader + node4.StableLeader) == 1 and (node0.FollowingStableLeader + node1.FollowingStableLeader + node2.FollowingStableLeader + node3.FollowingStableLeader + node4.FollowingStableLeader) == 4 )
query.comment: For all states, the only way the system can reach a deadlock is if one node has become a StableLeader and all other nodes are followers of that StableLeader. This makes the statespace finite and searchable, Otherwise the stable leader would continuously send heartbeats.

query.formula: A[] (node0.StableLeader + node1.StableLeader + node2.StableLeader + node3.StableLeader + node4.StableLeader) >= 0 and (node0.StableLeader + node1.StableLeader + node2.StableLeader + node3.StableLeader + node4.StableLeader) <= 1
query.comment: Invariantly, only one node can ever be a stable leader.
